title: Secret Network message origin verification bypass historical bridge incident

historical_exploit.attack_summary: An attacker exploited a vulnerability in Secret Network’s modified CW20-ICS20 contract used for the Axelar IBC bridge. By creating a fake Cosmos chain and sending forged IBC deposit packets (the contract had critical source-channel verification checks commented out), the attacker minted approximately $4.67 million in unbacked “saTokens” (Secret-wrapped versions of Axelar-bridged assets). These were redeemed through the legitimate bridge channel, draining real assets from Axelar’s escrow in about 18 minutes. Funds were then bridged out via Osmosis to Ethereum and mostly cashed out on exchanges. The incident was detected on June 17 and publicly disclosed on June 19. Axelar paused the Secret bridge routes; its core protocol and other chains were unaffected. No funds have been recovered.
historical_exploit.attacker_profile: External attacker or unprivileged caller as described by the SlowMist incident row; exact actor permissions must be re-verified before reporting.
preconditions: A bridge, cross-chain router, gateway, wrapped-asset minter, liquidity pool, or validator/proof path accepts attacker-controlled message, proof, signature, transaction, deposit, configuration, or accounting input.
The accepted input releases existing bridge-held value, mints unbacked wrapped assets, miscredits a deposit, or lets a caller move assets beyond entitlement.
The target mapping must be reachable without relying on privileged key compromise, DNS/frontend phishing, social-account compromise, or pure rug-pull behavior.

value_path.asset_or_reward: bridge-held assets, wrapped assets, bridge liquidity, withdrawals, refunds, or backed cross-chain liabilities
value_path.how_value_was_extracted: An attacker exploited a vulnerability in Secret Network’s modified CW20-ICS20 contract used for the Axelar IBC bridge. By creating a fake Cosmos chain and sending forged IBC deposit packets (the contract had critical source-channel verification checks commented out), the attacker minted approximately $4.67 million in unbacked “saTokens” (Secret-wrapped versions of Axelar-bridged assets). These were redeemed through the legitimate bridge channel, draining real assets from Axelar’s escrow in about 18 minutes. Funds were then bridged out via Osmosis to Ethereum and mostly cashed out on exchanges. The incident was detected on June 17 and publicly disclosed on June 19. Axelar paused the Secret bridge routes; its core protocol and other chains were unaffected. No funds have been recovered.
value_path.why_it_paid: The historical row describes real loss, attempted bridge theft, unbacked minting, unauthorized withdrawal, or bridge-accounting failure that can be useful only if BridgeIndex has a matching current live-value extraction path.

mapping.why_this_might_map: BridgeIndex exposes bridge deposits, bot-gas accounting, withdrawal creation/approval/execution, refunds, token/resource mappings, relayer voting, and proposal execution. This historical primitive is useful only if it maps to extracting current BNB/USDT ledger value or backed bridge liabilities from the deployed BridgeIndex system.

notes: SlowMist attack method: Smart Contract Vulnerability.
Selected for scanned because: forged IBC packets accepted because source-channel checks were missing
This seed is not proof that BridgeIndex is vulnerable; Workflow 7 must reject it unless the primitive maps to non-admin extraction of a current live ledger row.
